content.full_text:
State af California 
Memorandum 
To 
: File 
Date 
:Feb. 25, 1977 
From 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
Ken Finney 
Sub~~ Telephone conversation with Judge Rothenberg's bailiff. 
Judge Rothenberg's bailiff inquired about what filing obli-
gations Judge Rothenberg has based on the fact that she was 
recently appointed to the bench but has not yet assumed 
office. 
I responded that the Judge would have to file an 
Assuming Office Statement (Form 720) at least ten days prior 
to assuming office, but would not have to file an Annual 
Statement (Form 721) this year. 
I indicated that the Judge's 
first Annual Statement would be due on March I, 1978, and 
would cover the period commencing with the date that the 
Judge files her first Form 720 and closing with the date of 
December 31, 1977. 
KF: jo

sections.question_synthetic: What are the filing obligations for a judge who has been recently appointed to the bench but has not yet assumed office?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: A newly appointed judge must file an Assuming Office Statement (Form 720) at least ten days prior to assuming office, but is not required to file an Annual Statement (Form 721) in the year of appointment. The judge's first Annual Statement will be due March 1, 1978, and will cover the period from the date the Form 720 is filed through December 31, 1977.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is an internal memorandum rather than a formal advice letter; no formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present; information structured as narrative response to telephone inquiry; high confidence in question_synthetic and conclusion_synthetic as they are explicitly stated in the memorandum text.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What are the filing obligations for a judge who has been recently appointed to the bench but has not yet assumed office?

CONCLUSION: A newly appointed judge must file an Assuming Office Statement (Form 720) at least ten days prior to assuming office, but is not required to file an Annual Statement (Form 721) in the year of appointment. The judge's first Annual Statement will be due March 1, 1978, and will cover the period from the date the Form 720 is filed through December 31, 1977.
